title: Bleachers – “dirty wedding dress”
author: Tom Breihan
summary:

The post Bleachers – “dirty wedding dress” appeared first on Stereogum.
full_text:
Extremely busy mega-producer Jack Antonoff is back with his band Bleachers, and they'll release a new album called everyone for ten minutes this spring. Antonoff's wife Margaret Qualley starred in the video for their lead single "you and forever," and now they've followed that song with a rowdy, countrified yarn called "dirty wedding dress."

You know how you get married and there's paparazzi everywhere because Taylor Swift is one of your guests? That's at least some of what "dirty wedding dress" is about: "So we got married that August/ And the neighbors all lost their minds/ We had to board up all the windows and shoot out the drones/ We took that sadness right from Saturday night!" There's also some lyrics about critics, and I probably identify with those a little more.

Along the the new song, Bleachers have also unveiled plans for a big tour, but they're doing the thing where they don't name the actual venues they're playing. You can figure at least some of them out through context clues, though. Like: Bleachers are playing Columbia, Maryland, so if they're not hitting Toby's Dinner Theatre, then it's probably Merriweather Post Pavilion. You will find "dirty wedding dress" and those tour dates down below.

TOUR DATES:

everyone for ten minutes is out 5/22 via Dirty Hit. For the tour, pre-sale starts 3/18, with general on-sale beginning 3/20; sign up here.
